content:
# Matthew Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_014_page_001.jpg, MAT_014_page_002.jpg

1. In that season Herodes the tetrarch heard the report of the Yeshua

2. and said to the servants of him, "This is Ioannes the Baptizer; he was raised from the dead, and because of this the powers work in him."

3. For the Herodes, having seized the Ioannes, bound him and put [him] in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of Philippos the brother of him.

4. For the Ioannes was saying to him, "It is not lawful for you to have her."

5. And wishing to kill him, he feared the crowd, because as a prophet they held him.

6. But [on the] birthday of the Herodes happening, the daughter of the Herodias danced in the midst and pleased the Herodes,

7. from which with an oath he promised to give to her whatever she might ask.

8. And she, having been prompted by the mother of her, says, "Give to me here upon a platter the head of Ioannes the Baptizer."

9. And the king was grieved, but on account of the oaths and the [ones] reclining-together, he commanded [it] to be given.

10. And having sent, he beheaded the Ioannes in the prison.

11. And the head of him was brought upon a platter and was given to the girl, and she brought [it] to the mother of her.

12. And having come, the disciples of him took the body and buried him, and having come, they reported to the Yeshua.

13. And having heard, the Yeshua withdrew from there in a boat to a deserted place by himself. And having heard, the crowds followed him on foot from the cities.

14. And having come out, he saw [a] great crowd, and he was moved-with-compassion upon them and healed the sick of them.

15. And evening having come, the disciples came to him, saying, "Deserted is the place and the hour has already passed; dismiss the crowds, in order that having gone into the villages they may buy for themselves food."

16. And the Yeshua said to them, "They have no need to go; you give to them to eat."

17. And they say to him, "We do not have here except five loaves and two fish."

18. And he said, "Bring them here to me."

19. And having commanded the crowds to recline upon the grass, having taken the five loaves and the two fish, having looked up to the heaven, he blessed, and having broken, he gave the loaves to the disciples, and the disciples to the crowds.

20. And they all ate and were satisfied. And they took up the excess of the fragments, twelve baskets full.

21. And the [ones] eating were about five thousand men, apart from women and children.

22. And immediately he compelled the disciples to embark into the boat and to go before him to the other side, until he dismissed the crowds.

23. And having dismissed the crowds, he went up into the mountain by himself to pray. And evening having come, he was there alone.

24. And the boat was already many stadia from the land, being tormented by the waves, for the wind was contrary.

25. And in the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking upon the sea.

26. And the disciples, having seen him walking upon the sea, were troubled, saying, "It is a phantom!" And from the fear they cried out.

27. And immediately the Yeshua spoke to them, saying, "Take courage; I am; do not fear."

28. And answering him, the Petros said, "Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you upon the waters."

29. And he said, "Come." And having descended from the boat, Petros walked upon the waters and came toward the Yeshua.

30. But seeing the wind, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, he cried out, saying, "Lord, save me!"

31. And immediately the Yeshua, having stretched out the hand, took hold of him and says to him, "O [one] of little faith, why did you doubt?"

32. And of them having embarked into the boat, the wind ceased.

33. And those in the boat worshiped him, saying, "Truly, Son of God you are!"

34. And having crossed over, they came to the land, to Gennesaret.

35. And having recognized him, the men of the place that sent into that whole surrounding-region, and they brought to him all the [ones] having badly,

36. and they were urging him that they might only touch the fringe of the garment of him; and as many as touched were completely-healed.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **ἐγώ εἰμι (v.27):** "I am" — while contextually meaning "It is I," the Greek ἐγώ εἰμι echoes the divine name (Exodus 3:14 LXX). Walking on water (a divine prerogative in Job 9:8, Psalm 77:19) combined with "I am" creates a theophanic scene.

2. **φάντασμά (v.26):** "Phantom" — the disciples think they are seeing a ghost or apparition. This is the only use of this word in Matthew.

3. **v.19 — Eucharistic language:** "Having taken... having blessed... having broken... he gave" — these four verbs parallel the Last Supper narrative (26:26). The feeding miracle prefigures the Eucharist.

4. **v.33 — "Son of God":** The first time the disciples confess Jesus as Son of God in Matthew. This anticipates Peter's confession at Caesarea Philippi (16:16).

— EMET, AI Translator
